title: Regex Studio
description: A from-scratch regex workbench: parse to an AST, then take FOUR independent roads to one automaton — Thompson ε-NFA → subset construction, Brzozowski derivatives straight from the regex, Antimirov partial derivatives that build a tiny ε-free 'equation automaton' (collapsing (a|b|c)* to a single state where Thompson spends ten), and Glushkov's position automaton, ε-free with exactly one state per letter plus a start (the textbook (a|b)*abb → 6 states). All four minimise to the same canonical machine — itself reached two ways, by Moore and by Hopcroft's O(n log n) pass, cross-checked live. NEWEST — a FIFTH road that leaves the core algebra behind for the whole BOOLEAN CLOSURE of the regular languages: intersection (A&B), complement (~A) and difference (A−B). No ε-NFA can express these, so Thompson/Glushkov/Antimirov simply can't — but Brzozowski derivatives extend to them for free (∂c(A&B)=∂cA&∂cB, ∂c(~A)=~∂cA), so the derivative road builds an intersection or complement DIRECTLY into a complete DFA. The password lookahead (?=.*\d)(?=.*[a-z]).{6,} becomes a true regular intersection .*[0-9].*&.*[a-z].*&.{6,} with a finite DFA the lookahead form can never have; 'no abc substring' is ~(.*abc.*); identifiers-minus-keywords is a difference. Every extended pattern is proved the house way — live algebraic-law badges (involution, idempotence, excluded middle A∪~A≡Σ*, non-contradiction A∩~A≡∅), a CLASSICAL CROSS-CHECK that rebuilds the same language with the studio's product/complement automata and proves it equal, and a three-engine differential fuzzer (streaming derivative · derivative DFA · an independent span oracle defined straight from the algebra) over thousands of random Boolean expressions with zero disagreements. Run a string six ways — linear DFA, derivative DFA, streaming derivatives, the partial-derivative NFA, the position automaton, a linear-time Pike VM with captures, and a backtracking VM — and cross-check ten implementations (plus the platform's own RegExp) with a seeded differential fuzzer that already caught a real VM bug. Includes a static ReDoS analyser that proves catastrophic backtracking, synthesises the attack and prescribes the fix, equivalence checking with witnesses, language enumeration, DFA→regex synthesis, and DOT/SVG export. NEWEST of all — an ALGEBRA tab that builds the language's SYNTACTIC MONOID from scratch (the transition monoid of the minimal complete DFA), computes Green's relations R/L/J/H/D and draws the classic EGG-BOX diagram, finds the idempotents, and decides where the language sits by SCHÜTZENBERGER's theorem: aperiodic ⇔ STAR-FREE ⇔ first-order (FO[<]) definable ⇔ the DFA is COUNTER-FREE — cross-checked three independent ways, so (aa)* is exposed as the group ℤ/2 (not star-free, a real mod-2 counter) while a*b* is proved star-free and J-trivial (piecewise testable, Simon). The algebra now climbs a full VARIETY LADDER: trivial ⊂ piecewise-testable (J-trivial, Simon) ⊂ DA / two-variable FO²[<] / unambiguous polynomials (decided by 'every regular element is idempotent') ⊂ star-free / FO[<] / counter-free ⊂ all-regular — and it NAMES the syntactic group up to isomorphism straight off the Cayley table (cyclic ℤ/n, the abelian invariant-factor product ℤ/d₁×…×ℤ/dₖ incl. the Klein four ℤ/2×ℤ/2, dihedral Dₙ incl. S₃≅D₃, quaternion Q₈, A₄/S₄), so a(a|b)* lands in DA-but-not-piecewise, even-a-even-b is named the Klein four-group and (ab)* is the surprise: star-free yet NOT in DA. Click any egg-box cell or Cayley entry to light the state-map s↦δ(s,w) that element induces on the complete DFA — its fixed points, image rank and the cycle (counter) a group element does and an aperiodic one can't. A seeded fuzzer rebuilds tens of thousands of monoids (491,891 invariant checks across 48,000 patterns, zero disagreements) and confirms every ladder inclusion and group decomposition holds.
